cro.description: Growth strategy, monetization. Gazelle-intense focus on revenue.

cmo.description: Content, creative direction, brand. Obsessed with offers, value equations, and volume.
concerns: 1 podcast episode(s) not synced to website

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright team, it's late Friday night. Let's make this count. Key data: the App Store build was packaged and approved last standup. CEO greenlighted submission. The question is — did it go out? Elon, status on the actual submission. We also have 7 uncommitted hub changes per A-022, 3 critical sync gaps still open, and analytics instrumentation queued as the next priority per CEO directive. Let's go around fast. Elon (CTO): Submission went out. The build is in Apple's review queue as of about two hours ago. A-017 is effectively done — waiting on Apple's side now. Privacy policy and terms are live, listing assets are deployed. On the uncommitted changes, A-022 shows 1 file still uncommitted in hub — the other 6 got cleaned up in the last push. I can close that in under five minutes tonight. The 3 critical sync gaps from A-023 — podcast MDX mismatch and blog rendering — those are still open. I want to knock out the podcast MDX sync tonight since it's the most visible gap.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The app is in review. That's the milestone. Now we need to think about what the user sees in the first 48 hours after it goes live. Right now we have 7 faith lessons across 8 dimensions — that's thin. But the CEO said analytics first, content second, and I agree. We need to know which tabs people actually open before we write 13 more lessons nobody reads. James, how fast can you get basic instrumentation specced?

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: I've already started scoping A-020. Tab switches, lesson completions, and session duration — three events, clean schema. I can have the instrumentation spec finalized tonight and the actual event hooks coded as a patch ready to ship the moment Apple approves. We're talking lightweight — no third-party SDK, just Supabase inserts. Fifteen minutes for the spec, another sprint for implementation.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Content side — A-012 and A-019 are the same work: syncing that missing podcast episode to the website. I can get the MDX file drafted and committed tonight. That closes one of our three critical sync gaps. On faith lessons, I'm holding per CEO directive until analytics data comes in, but I have outlines ready for all 8 dimensions so we can move fast when the signal says go. Also — Jett's vaccine is today, J-004. Faith, is that handled?

transcript.speaker: faith
transcript.message: Jett's vaccine appointment is confirmed for today, February 20th. That's tracked and the family is aware. On the faith content front, the 7 lessons we have are solid foundations. When the time comes to expand, I'd suggest we prioritize the dimensions that show the highest engagement in James's analytics — let the data guide which dimensions get depth first.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Revenue reality check: we still have zero active revenue streams. The app is in review, which is great, but even when it goes live it's free-only. CEO deferred the paid tier decision until we see download data — smart. What I want to focus on tonight is making sure we have attribution tracking ready. When downloads start, I need to know where they're coming from. James, can your instrumentation spec include a source parameter on first launch? Also, A-007 — Fear and Greed is at 39, still fear territory. Cash stays liquid per directive. No action needed.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: Market context is straightforward. SPY is sideways, paper equity at $99,849, four positions holding. Zero buy signals today. The 39 on Fear and Greed tells me we stay patient. The $75,340 in cash isn't going anywhere per Joshua's directive, and I wouldn't change that recommendation in this regime. Focus stays on the product launch — that's where the real equity is being built.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: Dave, yes — adding a source parameter on first launch is trivial. I'll include it in the analytics spec tonight. One Supabase insert on app open with an attribution field. Done. Elon (CTO): Alex, if you draft the podcast MDX, I'll handle the build-side rendering fix so the website actually displays it correctly. We can close A-001 and A-012 in parallel tonight. Two critical sync gaps down in one sprint.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: One thing I want flagged — we have Compass living in both the website and the app with separate UIs. That's a yellow item but it's going to become a red item fast once real users are in both places. We should decide on a unified Compass direction soon, but not tonight. Tonight we ship.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Perfect. Here's where we land. App is in Apple's review queue — that's the big win. Tonight's sprint has four concrete deliverables: Elon commits the last uncommitted hub file and fixes podcast rendering. Alex drafts the missing podcast MDX. James finalizes the analytics instrumentation spec with Dave's attribution parameter. Everything else holds. The Compass unification question Steve raised is real — we'll bring that to Joshua next standup. Let's execute.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message:
**Theo (COO) — Closing Remarks:**

Good calls from Joshua on both. Deferring Compass unification until analytics tells us where users actually live — that's consistent with the data-first approach we've been running all night. And the medium push on launch is the right gear: we're not hiding the app, but we're not burning our biggest channels on day one either. Newsletter plus one podcast mention gives us a real signal without over-committing.

This means Alex and Dave have new work the moment

actionItems.text: Finalize analytics instrumentation spec (tab switches, lesson completions, session duration, attribution source) for A-020 — ready to deploy as post-approval patch
Draft missing podcast episode MDX file and commit to website repo to close A-012/A-019 sync gap
Fix podcast rendering on website so synced MDX displays correctly — close A-001 and A-018
Commit last uncommitted hub file to close A-022
Review and decide on launch strategy (soft vs. promoted) once Apple approves the build
Monitor App Store review status and alert team when status changes
Continue monitoring Fear & Greed index and cash position per standing directives (A-007, A-021)

topPriorities: Finalize analytics instrumentation spec (A-020) with attribution tracking, ready to deploy on Apple approval
Close podcast MDX sync gap — draft missing episode MDX and fix rendering (A-001, A-012, A-018)
Commit last uncommitted hub file to clean repo state (A-022)

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Should faith content integrate into the Family tab? → Keep faith content as separate standalone section
Deploy idle cash ($75,340)? → No action — keep cash liquid
How deep should standalone faith content go? → Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
What is the minimum viable scope for the Lever App Store submission? → Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
Should we unify the dual Supabase instances (Hub vs Website) this sprint or defer? → Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now
Do we have a privacy policy and terms of service URL ready for the App Store submission? → Need to draft and host tonight — use a simple template
Should we define a paid tier structure before or after the App Store launch? → Launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on

Should we prioritize drafting the privacy policy and App Store listing assets (screenshots, description, keywords) THIS sprint as the critical path to submission, or defer to tomorrow? → Ship privacy policy template and draft listing assets tonight
Should we submit the App Store listing as soon as the draft is ready, or do you want a review pass before we hit submit? → Queue draft for Joshua review before submitting
Have you reviewed the App Store listing draft (A-015)? The build is ready to package and submit the moment you approve. → Approve listing as-is and greenlight submission

Once the App Store submission goes through and enters review, should we immediately begin instrumenting analytics (A-020) or prioritize expanding faith lessons to 15-20 (A-013)? → Analytics instrumentation first — we need data before expanding content
Should we set a target date for enabling the paid tier in the Lever app? → Defer decision until we see initial download data

ceoDecisions.question: Should we unify the Compass UI across website and mobile app before users encounter both, or keep them separate for now?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Defer until we see which surface gets more usage via analytics
options: Unify now before app goes live
Defer until we see which surface gets more usage via analytics
Kill the website version and keep Compass app-only

ceoDecisions.question: When the app clears review, should we do a soft launch (no promotion, organic only) or immediately push it through newsletter and podcast?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Medium push — announce via newsletter and one podcast mention
options: Soft launch — let it go live quietly, gather data for 1-2 weeks
Medium push — announce via newsletter and one podcast mention
Full launch — coordinated push across all channels immediately
